meta.title: feat: add buildGreeting/shout utility (external Action proof)

overview.lead: A new utility module defines buildGreeting and shout, with strong input validation and consistent text transformation features.

bullets.title: buildGreeting function
bullets.body: Formats input as a greeting if it is a non-empty string; otherwise, throws an explicit error. Enforces strict validation of the input argument.

bullets.title: shout function
bullets.body: Converts any input to a string, uppercases it, and adds exclamation marks, allowing a broad range of inputs without throwing.

callouts.title: Explicit failure on invalid name input
callouts.body: buildGreeting's strict check means callers must guarantee a non-empty string or handle potential exceptions.

callouts.title: Broad input acceptance and coercion
callouts.body: shout's type coercion covers all input types, yielding a string for any value passed; review how non-string objects or special values are rendered.

clusters.name: Cluster A · Add buildGreeting and shout utility functions

files.riskLabel: New utility functions with basic type/format assumptions
files.why: Introduces new string utility helpers for greetings and uppercasing, intended for consumption elsewhere.
files.what: This adds two exported utility functions: buildGreeting(name), which validates that its argument is a non-empty string and formats it as a greeting, and shout(text), which coerces its input to a string, uppercases it, and adds exclamation marks. buildGreeting throws if its input is not a non-empty string, while shout is more permissive and always returns an uppercased string (with '!!!' appended) for any input. The risks are minor and revolve around type coercion and the explicit throw for bad input to buildGreeting.
before: // no equivalent -- this concept did not exist
after: // Small utility used by the WIRE-0 external-Action proof PR.
// Deliberately real code (not a docs-only change) so the pr-walkthrough
// pipeline has an actual diff to parse, cluster, and risk-assess.
throw new Error('buildGreeting: name must be a non-empty string');
return `Hello, ${name.trim()}! This walkthrough was generated by the public opensoyce-pr-walkthrough-action.`;

annos.text: A new file exports buildGreeting and shout, both utility functions. buildGreeting ensures the argument is a non-empty string and throws otherwise; shout outputs uppercased text for any input, using String coercion.

generationNotes: generator note: cluster badge raised from "lo" to "med" to match risk-list item "Input validation strictness in buildGreeting" (level="med"), which cites this exact file via entity_refs -- the two severity claims about the same file must agree.

risks.title: Input validation strictness in buildGreeting
risks.what: buildGreeting will throw an Error if its argument is not a non-empty string. This means any caller not ensuring type and emptiness prior to calling will encounter an exception, potentially affecting control flow or error handling paths.

risks.title: Type coercion behavior in shout
risks.what: shout converts its input to a string unconditionally (using String(text)), then applies uppercasing and appends '!!!'. Callers passing non-primitive or non-string values will receive coerced string results, which may differ from case-to-case but will not throw.

reading: Prior to this PR, no utility functions for greeting construction or shouting existed in the codebase: src/greeting.mjs was not present. The architecture lacked a standalone utility module for these string manipulations.

After the change, a new utility module (src/greeting.mjs) is added in the UTILITIES layer, supplying two functions: buildGreeting (which throws on invalid input) and shout (which stringifies and uppercases any input). This module is not yet consumed by other internal modules, but is designed for external import or use.

The addition creates two key new behaviors at the utility boundary: strict input enforcement in buildGreeting (risk-0) and unrestricted type coercion in shout (risk-1). The graph now includes src/greeting.mjs as a distinct supply point for other modules or external Actions to pull these features.

edges.label: imports utility functions
edges.claim: This relationship exposes both strict argument checking in buildGreeting and broad type coercion in shout to any external Action importing the utilities.

summary: This change introduces a new file (src/greeting.mjs) that exports two utility functions: buildGreeting and shout. buildGreeting formats a name as a greeting, enforcing that the argument is a non-empty string and throwing otherwise; shout uppercases any input (after coercion to string) and appends exclamation marks. Main risks are strict input enforcement in buildGreeting (which throws on bad input) and the type-coercion behavior of shout (which could yield unexpected string representations for some inputs).
